content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


December 29, 1994


John Miller
Environmental Planning Commissioner
196 Castro Street
Mountain View, California 94041

Re: Your Request For Advice
    Our File No. A-94-382

Dear Mr. Miller:

    We respond to your request for formal written advice of the
Fair Political Practices Commission regarding your potential
conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
Please note that the immunity conferred upon the requestor when
acting in accordance with the Commission's advice is prospective
only and nothing in this letter should be construed to apply to
past conduct. (See generally Section 83114 and Regulation 18329.)

QUESTION

    May you participate in matters before the Environmental
Planning Commission concerning the allowance of child care
facilities in industrial zones?

CONCLUSION

    Yes. It is not reasonably foreseeable that the decision
regarding child care centers in industrial zones would affect your
financial interests.

FACTS

    You are a member of the Environmental Planning Commission for
the City of Mountain View, California ("Environmental Planning
Commission."). You are also an architect, and you have previously
developed conceptual diagrams for the use of safe rooms for child
care centers in industrial zones. Your concept is not novel,
proprietary, nor unique. You have identified over twenty other

1         Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-382
Page 2


architects in the surrounding area with comparable levels of
expertise who are sufficiently competent to implement the
technology identified in your conceptual diagrams. These
architects are, like you, former employees of a large
architectural firm with experience in hazardous materials
containment.

     To date, no such safe rooms have been built or designed by
you or any of the other architects you've identified. The
Environmental Planning Commission is considering a proposal to
prohibit child care facilities within one thousand feet of
industrial zones where hazardous materials are stored. You have
proposed that such facilities may be allowed if they are
constructed with a safe room for sheltering people in place when
evacuation is not possible.

     You are not currently building, designing, or discussing the
possibility of applying your designs to any building in the City
of Mountain View or any other city.

                              ANALYSIS

     The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act prohibit a
public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise
attempting to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) An official
has a financial interest in a decision when it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect on, among other things, the official or his immediate
family, any investment interest worth one thousand dollars or
more, or any source of income of two hundred and fifty dollars or
more within the preceding twelve months. (Section 87103.)

     Consistent with the above definitions, a possible financial
effect will not disqualify an official from participating unless
that effect is reasonably foreseeable. Reasonably foreseeable
is a question of fact. An effect is reasonably foreseeable if it
is substantially likely to occur. An effect that is merely
possible does not cause disqualification; however, absolute
certainty is not required to trigger an official's
disqualification. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.)

     Under the facts presented, it is not reasonably foreseeable
that you will be financially affected by a decision whether to
allow child care facilities in industrial areas. The City of
Mountain View may decide to allow such facilities in industrial
areas only if they are built with safe rooms. Even in that
eventuality, there is only a mere possibility that you would
receive additional architectural income. As there are numerous
architects equally capable of undertaking the design of such
facilities, it is not substantially likely that you would be
financially affected in a material manner.

File No. A-94-382
Page 3


You have asked for guidelines on the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Act. We have enclosed a pamphlet explaining
these provisions in some detail. Please feel free to contact the
undersigned should you have any further questions regarding your
responsibilities under the Act.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          Steven G. Churchwell
                                          General Counsel
                                          [signature]
                                          By:  Daniel S. Muallem
                                               Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure




:P

Planning Commission concerning the allowance of childcare
facilities in industrial zones?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
It is not reasonably foreseeable that the decision

financial interests.
